en.extract:
The common scimitarbill (Rhinopomastus cyanomelas) is a species of bird in the family Phoeniculidae.
It is found in Angola, Botswana, Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Eswatini, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Namibia, Rwanda, Somalia, South Africa, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.


Description
Adults weigh roughly 35 grams and 26 cm in length. As adults, their beaks are black but as juveniles, their beaks are grey. Females and juveniles are more brown on the front of their body. They feed almost entirely on insects behind the bark of a tree, which is the reason for their probing beak.


References


External links
(Scimitarbilled woodhoopoe=) Common scimitarbill – Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''common scimitarbill''' ('''''Rhinopomastus cyanomelas''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Phoeniculidae]].
It is found in [[Angola]], [[Botswana]], [[Burundi]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Eswatini]], [[Kenya]], [[Malawi]], [[Mozambique]], [[Namibia]], [[Rwanda]], [[Somalia]], [[South Africa]], [[Tanzania]], [[Uganda]], [[Zambia]], and [[Zimbabwe]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Common Scimitarbill - eBird |url=https://ebird.org/species/cosbil1 |access-date=2025-08-24 |website=ebird.org |language=en}}</ref>

== Description ==
Adults weigh roughly 35 grams and 26 cm in length.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=DeWetsWild |date=2024-09-23 |title=Common Scimitarbill |url=https://dewetswild.com/2024/09/24/common-scimitarbill/ |access-date=2025-08-24 |website=DeWetsWild |language=en}}</ref> As adults, their beaks are black but as juveniles, their beaks are grey.{{cn|date=May 2026}} Females and juveniles are more brown on the front of their body.<ref name=":0" /> They feed almost entirely on [[Insect|insects]] behind the bark of a tree, which is the reason for their probing [[beak]].<ref name=":1" />
[[File:Rhinopomastus cyanomelas (Etosha).jpg|thumb|Juvenile in [[Etosha National Park]]]]
